player_notes.og anunoby: Diagnosed with a right hamstring strain, listed day-to-day and questionable for Knicks Game 3 vs. 76ers. If he misses or is limited, expect redistributed offensive load for Brunson, Hart, and Bridges.
player_notes.james harden: Back-to-back poor playoff performances — 3-for-13 FG, 0-for-4 from 3 in Game 2. Fade scoring and 3PM props until signs of offensive recovery emerge.
player_notes.karl-anthony towns: Foul trouble has been a notable issue; analysts calling him out specifically. Increased foul risk suppresses his minutes/rebound/point upside — factor into floor props.

game_notes.NYK vs PHI: OG Anunoby (hamstring strain) is day-to-day for Game 3. His absence would meaningfully shift Knicks wing usage and defensive assignments.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: No injury listed for Anunoby in the Playoff Injury Landscape or series diary heading into R2. Series diary notes his G6 performance (29/7/2, 11-14 FG, 4 3PM, 27 min) and flags his G6 efficiency must be evaluated cautiously given blowout context.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Multiple news items (Shams Charania, ESPN) report Anunoby has been diagnosed with a right hamstring strain and is day-to-day, questionable for Game 3 of the NYK-PHI R2 series.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add OG Anunoby to the Active With Documented Concerns section of the Playoff Injury Landscape: 'OG Anunoby (NYK) — Right hamstring strain, diagnosed post-R1. Day-to-day, questionable for R2 G3 (at PHI). Availability is the primary NYK variable; his absence would compress the wing depth that defined NYK's G5-G6 R1 closing run and shift offensive burden to Brunson and Bridges.'
